content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 10, 1993

Lynn Duncan
Administrative Assistant/Assistant Clerk of the Authority
San Francisco County Transportation Authority
255 City Hall
San Francisco, California 94102

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-090

Dear Ms. Duncan:

This is in reply to your request for advice under the
Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹.

QUESTION

The San Francisco County Transportation Authority (the
"Authority") Board of Directors consists of elected officials.
Under Section 89001 and Commission Regulation 18901, is it
permissible for the Authority to send a mass mailing of its
newsletter containing a list of the board members on the same page
as the masthead, a separate list, in the newsletter body, of
the Authority's committees and the board members who serve on each
committee?

CONCLUSION

If the roster listing of the officials on the newsletter's
masthead is a reproduction of the normal letterhead of the agency,
then it is permissible for the Authority to send a mass mailing of
a newsletter which lists the board members on the masthead page
and lists a roster of the Authority's committees in the body of
the newsletter. Otherwise, the Authority may include only one of
the lists in the newsletter.

___________________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-93-090
Page 2

ANALYSIS

Except in certain circumstances, Section 89001, as
interpreted and implemented by Regulation 18901, prohibits elected
officials and their agencies from sending mass mailings² at
government expense which feature those officials.

However, subdivision (b)(1) of Regulation 18901 permits the
sending of a mass mailing containing:

(1) Any item in which the elected
officer's name appears only in the letterhead
or logotype of the stationery, forms
(including "For Your Information" or
"Compliments of" cards), and envelopes of the
agency sending the mailing, or of a committee
of the agency, or of the elected officer, or
in a roster listing containing the names of
all elected officers of the agency. In any
such item, the names of all elected officers
must appear in the same type size, typeface,
type color, and location. Such item may not
include the elected officer's photograph,
signature, or any other reference to the
elected officer, except as specifically
permitted in this subdivision (b)(1) or
elsewhere in this regulation.

Furthermore, subdivision (b)(8) of Regulation 18901 permits
the sending of a mass mailing containing:

(8) A telephone directory, organization
chart, or similar listing or roster which
includes the names of elected officers as well
as other individuals in the agency sending the
mailing, where the name of each elected
officer and individual listed appears in the
same type size, typeface, and type color.
Such item may not include an elected officer's

_________________________

2 Section 82041.5 and Regulation 18901(a)(4) essentially define
"mass mailing" for purposes of Section 89001 as over 200
unsolicited and substantially similar items sent in a calendar
month.

Our File No. A-93-090
Page 3

photograph, name, signature, or any other
reference to an elected officer, except as
specifically permitted in this subdivision
(b)(8) or elsewhere in this regulation.

The specific issue your request presents is whether, under
subdivisions (b)(1) and (b)(8) of Regulation 18901, the
"letterhead" exception of the regulation applies to the names
listed in the masthead portion of the newsletter, and the "roster"
exception of the regulation applies to the committee listings in
the body of the newsletter.

We have previously advised that the "letterhead" exception in
subdivision (b)(1) does not apply exclusively to letters sent by
the agency. (Avance Advice Letter, No. 91-84.) Therefore, it is
permissible for an agency's newsletter to contain a listing of
elected officials affiliated with the agency if the agency's
normal letterhead is printed on the newsletter. On this basis, to
the extent that the listing of the Authority's board members on
the masthead page of the newsletter is a reproduction of the
Authority's normal letterhead, inclusion of the names on that page
of the newsletter is permissible.

Also, both subdivision (b)(1) and subdivision (b)(8) permit
the inclusion of a "roster" listing of the agency's elected
officials. The listing of the Authority's board members on the
masthead page would meet either of these exceptions even if the
listing is not a reproduction of the Authority's normal
letterhead.

More problematic is the listing of the board members on the
Authority's various committees in the body of the newsletter.
While we believe that this listing independently meets the
"roster" exception set forth in subdivision (b)(8), whether it can
be listed in the newsletter at all depends on the classification
of the roster listing on the masthead page. A roster listing of
elected officials in a mass mailing can only appear once in the
mailing. Thus, if the roster listing on the masthead page is not
a reproduction of the Authority's normal letterhead, but still
appears on the masthead page as proposed, the "roster" listing
exception will not apply to it and the committee roster listing in
the body of the newsletter will not be permitted.

Accordingly, you may include the masthead page roster listing
and the committee roster listing in the newsletter only if the
masthead page listing is a reproduction of the Authority's normal
letterhead. If it is not, then you may include only one of these
listings in the newsletter.

Our File No. A-93-090
Page 4


          I hope that this sufficiently addresses your question.  If
not, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Scott Hallabrin
                                             Senior Counsel

JM:SH:dl
